title: Continuity queue reconciliation is deterministic and non-mutating
summary: A committed reconciler now reads canonical continuity queue items and additive continuity updates as separate state layers, rejects foreign-team or mutation-claiming updates, flags duplicate or overlapping proposals, computes deterministic SHA-256 identities, and never writes the canonical queue. Five Node tests passed against byte-identical pre-commit source and test files.

status_relations.observed: The canonical queue still contains completed M-001 while M-002 exists only as an additive proposal; the reconciler preserves that distinction.
status_relations.inferred: A deterministic projection can make continuity work visible without silently converting proposals into canonical assignments.
status_relations.proposed: Use the projection output as a review artifact before any conflict-aware canonical queue update.
status_relations.superseded: The assumption that additive queue-update files either have no durable value or automatically mutate canonical state is superseded by an explicit two-layer projection.
status_relations.unresolved: No repository-level adoption process yet authorizes a conflict-free proposal to modify ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the reconciler and tests at their cited commits, run node --test operations/continuity/reconcile-queue-projection.test.mjs, and reject this record if canonical items are mutated, foreign-team updates are admitted, duplicate or overlapping proposals are silently resolved, or deterministic digests differ across equivalent key orderings.
